verdicts.note: The record marks exists='yes' with a URL to the /releases page. However, the evidence shows only an *announcement* of a 'DealCloud MCP Server client preview starting July 2026' (available only with 'DealCloud with Celeste' license). The /releases page is not itself an MCP server implementation or repository; it is a changelog. Per the instructions, a link to the app's own docs/homepage is NOT an MCP server. The MCP server is in limited preview, not a publicly available dedicated MCP server repo.
The URL points to the DealCloud API release notes page, not a dedicated MCP server repository or implementation. This should be removed or set to a proper MCP server repo if one exists.

The one_liner states 'official MCP server in client preview as of mid-2026' but the evidence clearly says 'client preview starting July 2026' (which is mid-2026, so timing is correct) BUT describes it as a feature available only with a 'DealCloud with Celeste' license, not a general MCP server. The characterization should reflect that this is a licensed feature in preview, not a generally available MCP server.

Evidence confirms OAuth2 client credentials flow (client_id, apiKey/client_secret, scopes, 900s token expiry) and that apiKey is used as the client_secret. The dual-method characterization (OAuth2 + API key) is accurate per the docs.

Evidence confirms no public self-serve signup; API keys are obtained via the user's DealCloud site admin (Admin > User Management, user profile). This matches 'admin-approval' and the notes about requiring an enterprise subscription.

Evidence clearly describes REST APIs organized into functional areas (User Management, Schema, Data, Backups, Publications, Relationship Intelligence) plus Swagger/OpenAPI spec. Type=REST, breadth=broad is well-supported.

'buildable-with-caveats' is appropriate: the API is functional and documented, but requires an existing enterprise subscription and admin-issued credentials. The main_blocker accurately describes these constraints.
All four evidence URLs are cited correctly and their 'supports' descriptions align with the content shown.
0.7 is reasonable given the enterprise-only access model and the MCP server being in preview (not GA).

Flags accurately reflect no free-tier, MCP server in preview (July 2026), and mention of third-party integrations. Evidence confirms the first two; third-party integrations are mentioned in flags but not in the evidence excerpts (acceptable as contextual notes).

revised.name: DealCloud
revised.one_liner: DealCloud is an enterprise deal/relationship CRM offering a broad REST API (data, schema, user management, publications, backups) secured via OAuth2 client credentials; MCP integration available via licensed Celeste feature in client preview as of July 2026.

auth_methods.notes: Client Credentials flow; POST client_id, apiKey/client_secret, and scope(s) to /api/rest/v1/oauth/token; returns bearer token valid 900 seconds, used in Authorization header.
apiKey used as the client_secret in the OAuth2 client credentials request; obtained via site admin (Admin > User Management, user profile).

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier or public self-serve signup; DealCloud is an enterprise CRM requiring an existing subscription/enterprise agreement. Developers get client_id/apiKey via their DealCloud site admin, not an open developer portal.

api_surface.notes: REST APIs organized into functional areas: User Management, Schema, Data, Backups, Publications, Relationship Intelligence, plus Swagger/OpenAPI spec exposed per-site.

revised.main_blocker: Requires an existing enterprise DealCloud subscription and admin-issued client_id/apiKey; no open self-serve developer signup. MCP integration available only via licensed 'DealCloud with Celeste' feature in limited client preview (July 2026).

evidence.supports: OAuth2 client credentials flow, scopes, 900s token expiry, Authorization header usage
Overview of REST API functional areas: auth, user management, schema, data, backups, publications, relationship intelligence; Swagger/Postman/VSCode tooling
Announcement of DealCloud MCP Server client preview starting July 2026, available as part of DealCloud with Celeste license

flags: No public free-tier/self-serve signup found — enterprise agreement required
MCP server feature requires separate 'DealCloud with Celeste' license and is in client preview (July 2026), not generally available
Third-party MCP integrations exist via Intapp Celeste (limited release) and Louisa AI
